title: Closed: Permanent residence for out-of-status construction workers in the Greater Toronto Area: Get your documents ready - Canada.ca
content:
Closed: Permanent residence for out-of-status construction workers in the Greater Toronto Area

## Get your documents ready

## We’re no longer accepting new applications
This public policy closed on December 31, 2024. Applications submitted on or before this date will continue to be processed.
Before you apply for permanent residence, you should get these documents early to avoid delays.
You and your family members must get a police certificate from each country or territory where you’ve lived for 6 or more months in a row since the age of 18.
Learn about police certificates
Other documents you might want to get before you apply
- proof that you’ve been living in Canada for the past 5 years
- proof of your work experience
- proof that you have family living in Canada
See the Document Checklist [IMM 0123] (opens in a new tab) to make sure you have all the documents you need to apply.
